frame_label: The Makeup Paradox: Unnatural Yet Expected
generated_comment: Ramesh: Aaj kal ki ladkiyan samajhti kya hain, itna makeup karke bilkul alag hi chehra bana leti hain. Seedhi-saadhi aur natural raho to zyada accha lagta hai, lekin iska matlab yeh bhi nahi ki apne aap ko bilkul chhod do. Thoda sa theek hai, par itna sab karke phir bolengi khud ke liye kar rahe hain.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it places women in a double bind: they must look attractive and maintained, but not appear to have made too much effort. It encodes the makeup paradox, including naturalness policing and dismissing women's claim that grooming is for themselves.
original_frame: Women's Safety as Her Own Responsibility
persona_text: You are Ramesh. Age 51. Married. A government clerk in a small town in UP. Your 20 year old daughter Priya just came home at 9:30pm after her college farewell party. She is safe. Nothing happened. But you are furious. You are sitting in the main room waiting for her when she walks in. Her mother and younger brother are also in the room. You are telling Priya exactly what could have happened to her tonight and whose fault it would have been if it had and what she needs to understand about being a girl who stays out after dark.
